title: [OBSERVABILITY]
text: [OBSERVABILITY] Use [OBSERVABILITY] section to control the behavior of the observability feature in Uniface. If the observability settings are not explicitly configured, the default settings are applied. Syntax [SETTINGS] Setting {{=} Value} For example: Explain this code [OBSERVABILITY] TRACE = on TRACE_ENDPOINT = http://localhost:4318/v1/traces Copy Arguments Setting : Name of the setting. Value : Value of the setting. If not specified, the default value is used. Refer to the individual assignment settings for their default values. Use Use in the assignment files of client applications, Uniface servers. Description The [OBSERVABILITY] section controls Uniface’s observability feature, which exports telemetry data, such as traces and logs, to compatible backends to the OpenTelemetry (OTel) standard. Traces provide insight into ProcScript execution flow and latency. Logs export putmess messages for debugging. Optional enrichment settings allow adding more data to spans. Span processing settings (queue size, batch size, schedule delay) help tune throughput and latency. Example: Enable Traces and Logs Explain this code [OBSERVABILITY] TRACE = on LOG = on TRACE_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/traces LOG_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/logs Copy Example: Set a different service name and service role Explain this code [OBSERVABILITY] TRACE = on LOG = on TRACE_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/traces LOG_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/logs SERVICE_NAME=Accounting SERVICE_ROLE=server Copy Example: Enable Traces with additional telemetry data to spans Explain this code [OBSERVABILITY] TRACE = on TRACE_ENDPOINT = http://yourhost:port/v1/traces ; Add $status as span attribute ADD_PROC_STATUS = on ; Add $proc_error as span attribute ADD_PROC_ERROR = on ; Add ProcScript profiling as span attributes ADD_PROCPROFILING = on ; Add ProcScript tracing as span events ADD_PROCTRACING = on Copy Configuration Settings Setting Value Type Default value Description TRACE Boolean Off Enable trace telemetry (on/off). LOG Boolean Off Enable logging telemetry (on/off). SERVICE_ROLE String Defaults depend on how the application is started, e.g.: Starting with uniface executable → defaults to client Starting with userver executable → defaults to server Otherwise → defaults to internal Description: Role of the service (client, server, internal). SERVICE_NAME String Uniface_App Logical name of your service (used for grouping spans/logs). TRACE_ENDPOINT String http://localhost:4318/v1/traces OTLP HTTP endpoint for traces. LOG_ENDPOINT String http://localhost:4318/v1/logs OTLP HTTP endpoint for logs. QUEUE_SIZE Numeric 2048 Max spans in the buffer. BATCH_SIZE Numeric 512 Max spans per batch. SCHEDULE_DELAY Numeric 5000 Time (ms) between exports. FLUSH_TIMEOUT Numeric 5000 Max time (ms) to flush spans on shutdown. ADD_PROC_STATUS Boolean Off Add $status value as Trace span attribute ADD_PROC_ERROR Boolean Off Add $proc_error value as Trace span attribute ADD_PROC_PROFILING Boolean Off Add ProcScript Profiling and Tracing data as Trace span attributes ADD_PROC_TRACING Boolean Off Add ProcScript Profiling and Tracing data as Trace span events. Note: This option is unavailable if your Uniface application is compiled with /nodebug. Enabling this may impact performance—use with caution. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
